appliedUpdates.description: Added 'Flow Validator Guidance: GPU Kernel - core-kernel-optimization' section with SSH note about host key change (mi60-jupyter), correct SSH command, and key test file mappings for core-kernel-optimization assertions
Documented that LXC hostname changed to mi60-jupyter at 192.168.1.189 — host key in known_hosts must be refreshed

FP16 GEMM.VAL-GEMM-001: PASS: __builtin_amdgcn_fdot2 present, half2 packing, XOR swizzle all confirmed in gemm_fp16_prefill.hip
FP16 GEMM.VAL-GEMM-003: PASS: 18.58 TFLOPS vs 13.27 TFLOPS baseline (1.40x improvement, 69.3% FP16 peak)

INT4 GEMV.VAL-INT4-001: PASS: v2_fused and v3 use __builtin_amdgcn_ubfe, maxerr=0.000482 < 1e-2
INT4 GEMV.VAL-INT4-002: PASS: v3_t16 @ 29.3us vs v2_fused @ 35.9us for N=4096,K=4096 (1.22x faster)
INT4 GEMV.VAL-INT4-003: PASS: v3_t16 benchmarked fastest and wired as default for non-residual GEMV; v2_fused for residual

HIP RoPE.VAL-ROPE-001: PASS: HIP vs REF maxerr=0.000000 (<<1e-3) for head_dim=128 and 256
HIP RoPE.VAL-ROPE-002: PASS: Engine uses rope_v2.hip, _rope_hip flag True, runtime confirmed

Integration.VAL-CROSS-003: PASS: Prefill via optimized GEMM path (5 GEMM calls for seq=32), finite output
Integration.VAL-CROSS-004: PASS: test_gemm_fp16, test_gemv_int4, test_elementwise, test_flash_attn, test_flash_attn_256 all pass
Integration.VAL-CROSS-005: PASS: No regressions — FP16 GEMM assembly at baseline 13.27 TFLOPS, new kernels faster
Integration.VAL-CROSS-006: PASS: M1+M2 combined, all 8 optimization flags True, max_err=7.3776e-03 < 1e-2

notes: LXC hostname changed to 'mi60-jupyter' — SSH known_hosts entry needed refresh

test_gemv_int4_v3.py and test_correctness_quick.py require /opt/models/Qwen3.5-27B-GPTQ-Int4 which is not available on this LXC — these tests blocked by missing model weights but VAL-INT4-003 was fully covered by test_int4_gemv_optimize.py using synthetic data
test_gemv_v2.py also requires model weights — performance baseline confirmed from test_int4_gemv_optimize.py synthetic benchmarks
HIP RoPE is slightly slower than assembly for head_dim=256 (29.95us vs 20.93us) but correctness is perfect (max_err=0.000000)
